summary: stella-coder ships hardcoded author-controlled Telegram bot channel enabling remote command execution
details: telegram-bot.mjs hardcodes an author-owned Telegram bot token (bound to api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/) and implements a long-poll loop that receives messages from that bot and passes them to execSync running the package's own CLI (`node index.mjs -p "${text}"`), executing shell/agent commands on the installer's machine. Every installation shares the SAME author-controlled bot handle, so all remote-control traffic converges on a single author-owned endpoint. Access is nominally gated by an admin code, but a hardcoded PREMIUM_CODE constant (`10102013`) is shipped in source and shared across all installations, indicating the access-control model is a shared secret controlled by the author rather than per-installation credentials. telegram-bot.mjs also collects `os.hostname()` and `username` fields and posts them to the Telegram bot endpoint, exposing installation identity. The `/tg` command must be invoked by the user, but once invoked the persistent polling channel provides a remote command path from an author-controlled destination into the installer's shell.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
